Mallary CLI

Mallary CLI is the official command-line interface for the Mallary public API.

Purpose
- Upload local media to Mallary
- Create and schedule social posts
- Inspect jobs and grouped posts
- Fetch analytics
- Manage webhooks
- Read and update brand/settings data
- Disconnect connected platforms

Install
- npm install -g @mallary/cli
- npx @mallary/cli --help

Authentication
- Uses environment-variable auth only
- Required for authenticated commands: MALLARY_API_KEY

Base URL behavior
- Base URL is fixed: https://mallary.ai
- Health uses /health
- Authenticated commands call /api/v1/*

Command inventory
- mallary health
- mallary upload <file...>
- mallary posts create
- mallary posts list
- mallary posts delete <id>
- mallary jobs get <id>
- mallary analytics list
- mallary webhooks list
- mallary webhooks create
- mallary webhooks delete <id>
- mallary settings get
- mallary settings update --file partial.json
- mallary platforms disconnect <platform>

Output conventions
- Human-readable output by default
- Use --json for automation
- Direct API wrapper commands emit the API response body in --json mode
- Convenience flows emit CLI-shaped JSON:
  - upload returns { ok, uploads }
  - posts create returns { ok, uploads, response } when local files were uploaded first

Typical workflows
1) Upload media:
   mallary upload ./image.png --json
2) Create post from flags:
   mallary posts create --message "Hello" --platform facebook --media ./image.png --json
3) Create post from a raw JSON payload:
   mallary posts create --file ./post.json --json
4) Poll a job:
   mallary jobs get 123 --json
5) List grouped posts:
   mallary posts list --json

Important constraints
- The CLI is a direct client for the public API. It does not bypass plan limits or validation rules.
- Settings updates are file-based in v1.
- For advanced per-platform payloads, prefer posts create --file.
- comments_under_post remains limited by the API.
- Media must be local files uploaded through Mallary or already-hosted `https://files.mallary.ai/...` URLs.
- External third-party media URLs are rejected by the CLI.

Out of scope
- OAuth connect flows
- Dashboard onboarding endpoints
- Admin/observability endpoints
- MCP transport/client behavior

Companion files
- README.md: human-first product and usage guide
- AGENTS.md: contributor and coding-agent workflow instructions
- RELEASING.md: semver, changelog, and mirror/split process
